skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Creates detailed implementation plans from requirements before coding begins.
skill.summary: Turn requirements into step-by-step engineering plans with files, tests, commands, and handoff guidance.
skill.author: Cycleaddict

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command-like Markdown and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found no executable code, cryptographic operations, network access, or prompt injection. The command examples are documentation for implementation plans and git/test workflows, so the residual risk is low and publication is acceptable.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The command-like text appears inside Markdown planning instructions and examples. It does not execute commands, spawn shells, or process user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is Markdown content in SKILL.md, including inline paths, pytest examples, and git examples. No executable script file or runtime command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain skill metadata, planning assumptions, and a goal placeholder. No hash function, cipher, credential handling, or cryptographic API is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines were reviewed directly and contain only prose or Markdown placeholders. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms or cryptographic behavior.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering tasks often fail because the plan is too vague. This skill turns requirements into precise task plans with files, tests, commands, and execution handoffs.
actual_capabilities: Converts requirements into bite-sized implementation tasks.
Specifies files to create, modify, and test for each task.
Includes test-first steps with expected outcomes.
Adds exact commands that engineers can run during implementation.
Creates a handoff section for choosing an execution workflow.
Encourages small commits after verified task completion.
limitations: It does not inspect the codebase unless the assistant gathers context separately.
It does not execute the generated plan.
It depends on accurate requirements from the user.
It may need adjustment for teams that do not use test-first workflows.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Create a structured plan before adding a new product feature.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Team Handoff
use_cases.description: Document implementation steps so another developer can continue with less context.

use_cases.title: Clarify Test Work
use_cases.description: Break vague requirements into testable tasks with expected verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feature Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a short feature request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for this feature: [describe feature]. Include files, tests, commands, and expected results.

prompt_templates.title: Bug Fix Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a defect needs careful reproduction and verification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a test-first implementation plan for this bug: [describe bug]. Include reproduction steps, failing tests, minimal fix steps, and commit points.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when changing structure without changing behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactor implementation plan for [component or module]. Preserve behavior, identify tests, list files, and include rollback checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-System Delivery Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when work spans multiple services, packages, or teams.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an advanced implementation plan for [initiative]. Include architecture, phased tasks, dependencies, test strategy, documentation updates, and execution handoff options.

output_examples.input: Plan a new export feature for customer reports.
output_examples.output: A plan with a goal, architecture notes, task list, file targets, failing test steps, implementation steps, verification commands, and commit checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Plan a bug fix for duplicate notification emails.
output_examples.output: A focused plan that starts with reproduction, adds a failing test, narrows the fix location, verifies behavior, and documents the release note.

output_examples.input: Plan a refactor of authentication middleware.
output_examples.output: A staged plan that preserves behavior, maps affected files, identifies regression tests, separates mechanical moves, and adds review checkpoints.

best_practices: Provide the requirements, constraints, and known affected files before asking for a plan.
Ask for test-first tasks when behavior can be verified automatically.
Review the generated plan for missing domain rules before implementation starts.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for reading the codebase.
Do not accept placeholder file paths when exact paths are needed.
Do not skip verification steps because the plan looks complete.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It writes implementation plans that can include code examples, tests, commands, and handoff steps.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it before coding when a task has requirements, multiple steps, or meaningful testing needs.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The planning format is plain Markdown and is suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it require a specific programming language?
faq.answer: No. The examples are generic, and the plan should adapt to the project language and toolchain.

faq.question: Why does it mention frequent commits?
faq.answer: Frequent commits make each completed task easier to review, test, and revert if needed.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only documentation examples of commands and no executable malicious behavior.
